vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #1668

plan.title: Installer should validate Python 3.11+ and python3 availability before install (Windows)

narratives.Description: On Windows the Go installer reports success but leaves a broken environment because it never validates the Python interpreter the framework depends on. Two gaps: (1) it does not verify Python >= 3.11 (framework scripts use `from datetime import UTC`, which is 3.11+), so an older interpreter crashes the post-install doctor handoff and the pre-commit hooks; (2) interpreter resolution assumes `python3`, which is usually absent on Windows (only `python.exe` / the `py` launcher exist). This story adds an install-time Python preflight (version + interpreter resolution) and makes the doctor handoff degrade gracefully, so the failure is surfaced up front with an actionable message instead of as a raw traceback on the user's first commit.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
- Add a Python preflight to the installer CLI (cmd/deft-install): probe for a usable interpreter across `DEFT_PYTHON`, `python`, `python3`, and the Windows `py` launcher; run `<interp> --version`; require >= 3.11. On failure, abort (or, under --yes, fail loud) with an actionable message naming the 3.11+ requirement and the `DEFT_PYTHON` override.
- Surface the resolved interpreter: document `DEFT_PYTHON` prominently in installer output and prefer the resolved interpreter where the hooks are wired. The .githooks/pre-commit and pre-push hook scripts already fall back DEFT_PYTHON -> python3 -> python; extend the probe order to include the `py` launcher and guard against the Windows App-Execution-Alias stub for `python3`.
- Make the post-install doctor handoff in cmd/deft-install/setup.go detect an incompatible/missing interpreter and emit a concise actionable message instead of streaming a raw ImportError traceback to stdout.
- Add Go tests in cmd/deft-install covering version-too-old rejection, interpreter resolution order (incl. py launcher / DEFT_PYTHON), and the graceful doctor-handoff path.
narratives.UserStory: As a Windows user installing deft, I want the installer to verify a compatible Python (3.11+) and resolve the interpreter up front, so that I get a clear actionable error at install time instead of a broken commit workflow and a raw traceback later.
narratives.Approach: 2026-06-16 codebase verification (cmd/deft-install/setup.go + .githooks/): Problem 1 (no >=3.11 validation) is UNADDRESSED -- probeCoreToolsMissing/EnsureCoreTools only check interpreter PRESENCE via LookPath (coreToolCandidates maps python -> [python, python3]); there is no `--version` / >=3.11 gate, and although pyproject declares requires-python>=3.11 the Go installer does not enforce it. Problem 2 is PARTIALLY mitigated: the git hooks now resolve DEFT_PYTHON -> python3 -> python, but they do NOT probe the `py` launcher and remain exposed to the Windows App-Execution-Alias `python3` stub. doHandoffToDoctor hardcodes python/python3 and pipes stderr to stdout, so an incompatible interpreter still dumps a raw traceback. Net: issue is still valid; scope = installer-side preflight + interpreter resolution + graceful handoff.
narratives.Overview:
Here's a GitHub issue draft focused on the Python environment checks:

---

**Title:** Installer should validate Python 3.11+ and `python3` availability before install (Windows)

## Summary
On Windows, the Deft/Directive installer completes "successfully" but leaves the environment in a broken state because it never verifies the Python interpreter it depends on. Two distinct problems surface:

1. **Python version is too old** — framework scripts require Python **3.11+** but the installer runs against whatever Python is found, producing a hard crash.
2. **`python3` is not on PATH** — standard on Windows (only `python.exe` exists), which breaks the git hooks the installer wires up.

Both are detectable up front and should fail (or warn) at install time rather than during the post-install doctor handoff or on the user's first commit.

## Environment
- OS: Windows
- Installed Python at time of failure: 3.10 (`...\Python\Python310\`), later 3.13
- Deft/Directive: v0.48.0

## Problem 1 — Python version not checked (requires 3.11+)
Several framework scripts use `from datetime import UTC, datetime`, which only exists in Python 3.11+. With Python 3.10 the post-install doctor handoff crashes:

```
--- Doctor handoff (post-install state via --session --full prose) ---
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "...\.deft\core\scripts\doctor.py", line 31, in <module>
    from datetime import UTC, datetime
ImportError: cannot import name 'UTC' from 'datetime' (...\Python310\lib\datetime.py)
```
The same failure later occurs in `policy.py` / `preflight_branch.py` during the pre-commit hook:

```
File "...\.deft\core\scripts\preflight_branch.py", line 38, in <module>
    from policy import (...)
File "...\.deft\core\scripts\policy.py", line 29, in <module>
    from datetime import UTC, datetime
ImportError: cannot import name 'UTC' from 'datetime' (...\Python310\lib\datetime.py)
```
Upgrading to Python 3.11+ (user ended on 3.13) resolves it.

## Problem 2 — `python3` not available on PATH (Windows)
After install, the first commit fails because the git hooks invoke `python3`, which doesn't exist on a standard Windows Python install (only `python` / `python.exe`):

```
$ git commit -m "deft/directive installed"
Python was not found; run without arguments to install from the Microsoft Store,
or disable this shortcut from Settings > Apps > Advanced app settings > App execution aliases.
```
The installed hooks (`.githooks/pre-commit`) run:
- `preflight_branch.py` (branch protection)
- `verify_encoding.py` (encoding verification)
- `verify_vbrief_conformance.py` (vBRIEF conformance)

The `DEFT_PYTHON` env var works as a workaround:
```
DEFT_PYTHON = C:\Users\<user>\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python313\python.exe
```
But many Windows users will hit this, since `python3` on PATH is the exception, not the norm.

## Suggested fixes
- **Preflight check in the installer**: detect a usable Python interpreter and verify version is **>= 3.11** before proceeding; abort with a clear, actionable message (and link/instructions) if not.
- **Resolve the interpreter explicitly**: don't assume `python3` exists. Probe `python`, `python3`, the `py` launcher, and `DEFT_PYTHON`, then bake the resolved path into the hooks (or surface `DEFT_PYTHON` setup as a documented, prompted step rather than something users must discover).
- **Document `DEFT_PYTHON`** prominently in installer output and docs as the supported Windows override.
- **Make the doctor handoff degrade gracefully** instead of dumping a raw traceback when the interpreter is incompatible.

## Impact
A successful-looking install leaves a broken commit workflow until the user independently diagnoses (a) the 3.11+ requirement and (b) the missing `python3`/`DEFT_PYTHON` situation. Both are cheap to detect at install time.

narrative.Acceptance: Given a resolved Python interpreter older than 3.11, when deft-install runs, then it aborts (or, under --yes, fails loud) with an actionable message naming the 3.11+ requirement and the DEFT_PYTHON override, rather than reporting success.

Given a Windows host where only python.exe (or the py launcher) exists, when the installer and the installed git hooks resolve Python, then they find a working interpreter (probing DEFT_PYTHON, python, python3, and the py launcher) without depending on python3 being on PATH.

items.title: Doctor handoff degrades gracefully on an incompatible interpreter

narrative.Acceptance: Given an incompatible or missing interpreter, when the post-install doctor handoff runs, then it emits a concise actionable message instead of a raw ImportError traceback.
Given the installer runs, when it reports Python status, then DEFT_PYTHON is documented as the supported Windows override in the output.

items.title: Go tests cover preflight + resolution + handoff

narrative.Acceptance: Given the test suite, when `go test ./cmd/deft-install/...` runs, then it covers version-too-old rejection, interpreter resolution order, and the graceful doctor-handoff path.

expected_outputs: installer rejects Python < 3.11 with an actionable message naming the DEFT_PYTHON override
interpreter resolution probes DEFT_PYTHON/python/python3/py launcher in installer and git hooks
doctor handoff degrades gracefully without a raw traceback on an incompatible interpreter
passing Go tests covering version rejection, resolution order, and the handoff path
